---
title: "MakeoverMonday: Global Oil Production - Who Still Moves the Map"
description: "Country-level oil output in terawatt-hours from Our World in Data: top producers, trajectories since 1990, and shifting shares of the world total"
date: "2026-04-27"
x-posted: false
author: "chokotto"
categories:
  - MakeoverMonday
  - Python
  - Data Viz
source-topic: "Global Oil Production"
source-url: "https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/oil-production-by-country"
image: "thumbnail.svg"
code-fold: true
twitter-card:
  card-type: summary_large_image
  image: "thumbnail.png"
---

## Overview

Oil output remains a core lens on geopolitics and the energy transition. This makeover uses Our World in Data's country-level series (Energy Institute and Shift Data Portal, harmonised by OWID) in **terawatt-hours** (energy-equivalent) to compare how the largest producers evolved since 1990.

- **Data Source**: [Our World in Data - Oil production](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/oil-production-by-country)
- **Angle**: Absolute trajectories for the current top producers, then each country's share of annual world production

## Dataset

```{python}
#| label: load-packages
#| message: false

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
```

```{python}
#| label: load-data
#| message: false

url = (
    "https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/oil-production-by-country.csv"
    "?v=1&csvType=full&useColumnShortNames=false"
)
raw = pd.read_csv(url, storage_options={"User-Agent": "chokotto-makeover/1.0"})
raw.columns = raw.columns.str.replace(" ", "_")

oil = raw.rename(columns={"Oil": "oil_twh"}).copy()
oil = oil[oil["Year"] >= 1990]
oil = oil[oil["Entity"].notna()]
# Drop aggregates that would double-count
exclude = {"World", "Africa", "Asia", "Europe", "North America", "South America", "Oceania"}
oil = oil[~oil["Entity"].isin(exclude)]

latest_y = int(oil["Year"].max())
top_entities = (
    oil[oil["Year"] == latest_y]
    .nlargest(8, "oil_twh")["Entity"]
    .tolist()
)

by_year_total = oil.groupby("Year", as_index=False)["oil_twh"].sum()
by_year_total = by_year_total.rename(columns={"oil_twh": "world_total"})

top8 = oil[oil["Entity"].isin(top_entities)].merge(by_year_total, on="Year")
top8["share_pct"] = np.where(
    top8["world_total"] > 0, top8["oil_twh"] / top8["world_total"] * 100, 0
)
```

## My Makeover

```{python}
#| label: shared-style
#| include: false

THEME = dict(
    template="plotly_white",
    paper_bgcolor="white",
    plot_bgcolor="#f8fafc",
    font=dict(family="Inter, system-ui, sans-serif", color="#1e293b"),
)

NOTE_TEXT = "Oil production incl. crude, oil sands, NGLs; energy unit TWh (OWID methodology)"
SOURCE_TEXT = "Our World in Data (Energy Institute; Shift Data Portal)"
SOURCE_NOTE = f"<i>Note: {NOTE_TEXT}  |  Source: {SOURCE_TEXT}  |  (c) 2026 chokotto</i>"

PAL = ["#b45309", "#0f766e", "#1d4ed8", "#7c3aed", "#be123c", "#64748b", "#ca8a04", "#0369a1"]

def make_title(text):
    return dict(text=text, font=dict(size=15, color="#1e293b"), x=0, xanchor="left")

def add_legend_note(fig, text):
    margin_t = fig.layout.margin.t if fig.layout.margin.t is not None else 80
    if margin_t < 100:
        fig.update_layout(margin=dict(t=100))
    fig.add_annotation(
        text=text, xref="paper", yref="paper",
        x=0, y=1.0,
        xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
        showarrow=False,
        font=dict(size=11, color="#64748b"),
        align="left",
    )

def add_source(fig, position="bottom"):
    if position == "top":
        h = fig.layout.height or 450
        mt = fig.layout.margin.t if fig.layout.margin.t is not None else 80
        mb = fig.layout.margin.b if fig.layout.margin.b is not None else 80
        plot_h = h - mt - mb
        ABOVE_PX = 15
        y = 1.0 + (ABOVE_PX / plot_h) if plot_h > 0 else 1.05
        fig.add_annotation(
            text=SOURCE_NOTE, xref="paper", yref="paper",
            x=0, y=y, showarrow=False,
            font=dict(size=10, color="#94a3b8", style="italic"),
            align="left", xanchor="left", yanchor="bottom",
        )
        return
    TARGET_PX = 65
    ANNOT_HEIGHT_PX = 35
    h = fig.layout.height or 450
    mt = fig.layout.margin.t if fig.layout.margin.t is not None else 80
    mb = fig.layout.margin.b if fig.layout.margin.b is not None else 80
    min_mb = TARGET_PX + ANNOT_HEIGHT_PX
    if mb < min_mb:
        mb = min_mb
        fig.update_layout(margin=dict(b=mb))
    plot_h = h - mt - mb
    y = -(TARGET_PX / plot_h) if plot_h > 0 else -0.30
    fig.add_annotation(
        text=SOURCE_NOTE, xref="paper", yref="paper",
        x=0, y=y, showarrow=False,
        font=dict(size=10, color="#94a3b8", style="italic"),
        align="left", xanchor="left", yanchor="top",
    )

def assert_no_title_overlap(fig):
    height = fig.layout.height or 450
    margin_t = fig.layout.margin.t if fig.layout.margin.t is not None else 80
    plot_top_paper = 1.0
    for ann in (fig.layout.annotations or []):
        if getattr(ann, "yref", None) == "paper" and ann.y is not None:
            if ann.y > plot_top_paper:
                required_t = int((ann.y - 1.0 + 0.06) * height) + margin_t
                if required_t > margin_t:
                    fig.update_layout(margin=dict(t=required_t))
```

### The same eight producers still dominate volume, but ranks drift

```{python}
#| label: chart-1
#| fig-width: 10

color_map = {e: PAL[i % len(PAL)] for i, e in enumerate(top_entities)}

fig = go.Figure()
for entity in top_entities:
    sub = oil[oil["Entity"] == entity].sort_values("Year")
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=sub["Year"],
        y=sub["oil_twh"],
        mode="lines",
        name=entity,
        line=dict(color=color_map[entity], width=3 if entity == top_entities[0] else 2),
        hovertemplate=f"{entity}<br>%{{x}}: %{{y:,.0f}} TWh<extra></extra>",
    ))

lead = top_entities[0]
lead_last = oil[(oil["Entity"] == lead) & (oil["Year"] == latest_y)]["oil_twh"].sum()

fig.update_layout(
    **THEME,
    title=make_title(
        f"Oil output (TWh): {lead} led with {lead_last:,.0f} TWh in {latest_y} among top 8"
    ),
    height=450,
    margin=dict(l=60, r=30, t=100, b=100),
    xaxis=dict(title=""),
    yaxis=dict(title="Oil production (terawatt-hours)"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
)

add_legend_note(fig, "Top 8 countries by output in the latest year; lines from 1990")
add_source(fig)
assert_no_title_overlap(fig)
fig.show()

try:
    fig.write_image("chart-1.png", width=1200, height=600, scale=2)
except Exception:
    pass
```

### Share of world production: concentration vs diversification

```{python}
#| label: chart-2
#| fig-width: 10

years = sorted(top8["Year"].unique())
wide = top8.pivot(index="Year", columns="Entity", values="share_pct").reindex(years)
wide = wide.fillna(0)

fills = [
    "rgba(180,83,9,0.35)", "rgba(15,118,110,0.32)", "rgba(29,78,216,0.28)",
    "rgba(124,58,237,0.26)", "rgba(190,18,60,0.24)", "rgba(100,116,139,0.22)",
    "rgba(202,138,4,0.2)", "rgba(3,105,161,0.2)",
]
fig2 = go.Figure()
for i, entity in enumerate(top_entities):
    if entity not in wide.columns:
        continue
    fig2.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=wide.index,
        y=wide[entity],
        name=entity,
        stackgroup="one",
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(width=0.5),
        fillcolor=fills[i % len(fills)],
        hovertemplate=f"{entity}: %{{y:.1f}}%<extra></extra>",
    ))

fig2.update_layout(
    **THEME,
    title=make_title(
        "Share of estimated world oil output: eight largest producers (100% stacked)"
    ),
    height=450,
    margin=dict(l=60, r=30, t=100, b=100),
    xaxis=dict(title=""),
    yaxis=dict(title="Percent of summed country totals", range=[0, 100]),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, xanchor="right", x=1),
)

add_legend_note(
    fig2,
    "World total = sum of country-level series in dataset (excludes regional aggregates)"
)
add_source(fig2)
assert_no_title_overlap(fig2)
fig2.show()
```

***

_This post is part of [Makeover Monday](https://makeovermonday.co.uk/)._
